title: aira caryophyllea var. aggregata
intro: Aira caryophyllea var. aggregata, also known as Wood Meadow-grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can provide antioxidant and antiseptic benefits. Aira caryophyllea var. aggregata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits and medicinal properties.
